sentence: كِيف حَالَك الْيَوم؟ إِن شَاء اللَّه بِخَير.
raw: كيف حالك اليوم؟ إن شاء الله بخير.
ipa: ˈkiːf ˈħaːlak alˈjawm ˈʔin ˈʃaːʔ ˈallah biˈxajr
gloss_en: How are you today? Well, God willing.
notes: pan-Mashriqi kīf ḥālak greeting (brustad2000; holes2004); ق→q and aw retained (al-yawm) are node rank-1

gloss_en: The weather is hot today and the humidity is high.
notes: ج→dʒ node rank-1 (versteegh2014); geminates جّ رّ; sun assimilation ar-r

gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I want tea.
notes: pan-Mashriqi arīd 'I want' (brustad2000); ق→q retained (qahwa) is node rank-1; negation mā

gloss_en: I bought three fish from the market.
notes: ث→t stop reflex environment; ق→q (sūq); number; sun assimilation as-s; sedentary interdental→stop merger and qeltu qāf per (watson2002)

gloss_en: This man is looking for work.
notes: ج→dʒ node rank-1 (holes2004); geminates جّ وّ; hāḏa demonstrative

gloss_en: Go get the thing from the car.
notes: imperatives rūḥ/jīb; ج→dʒ node rank-1; geminate يّ; sun assimilation as-s; ǧīm→[dʒ] (Bedouin/rural Levant–Mesopotamia) per (watson2002)

sentence: الْجَوّ مُو حَارّ الْيَوم وَالسَّمَا مْغَيِّمَة.
raw: الجو مو حار اليوم والسما مغيمة.
ipa: alˈdʒaww ˈmuː ˈħaːrr alˈjawm ˈwassamaː ˈmɣajjima
gloss_en: The weather is not hot today and the sky is cloudy.
notes: pan-Mashriqi negator mū (brustad2000); geminates جّ يّ; aw retained

gloss_en: How much is the kilo? Five and a half.
notes: bikam question; geminate صّ; number; emphatic ص; Mashriqi emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: The appointment is at half past three in the afternoon.
notes: sun assimilation as-s; geminate صّ; emphatic ص; ق absent; Mashriqi sun-assimilation/emphatic phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: My elder brother lives in the city.
notes: long vowels; pan-Mashriqi axūy; ق absent here; Eastern short-vowel retention and vowel phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Go straight and then turn right.
notes: imperative rūḥ; aw/ay retained (baʕdayn); geminate فّ; ق absent; diphthong /aj aw/ retention per (watson2002)

sentence: الْأَكِل كَان طَيِّب بَسّ الشُّورْبَة كَانَت بَارْدَة.
raw: الأكل كان طيب بس الشوربة كانت باردة.
ipa: ˈalʔakil ˈkaːn ˈtˤɑjjib ˈbass aʃˈʃuːrba ˈkaːnat ˈbaːrda
gloss_en: The food was good but the soup was cold.
notes: geminate سّ; sun assimilation aš-š; emphatic ط in ṭayyib; Mashriqi emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Where did you put the key? I did not find it.
notes: pan-Mashriqi wēn (brustad2000); geminate طّ; negation mā; emphatic ط

gloss_en: How many came yesterday?
notes: ج→dʒ node rank-1 (versteegh2014); kam + number; ta_marbuta

gloss_en: If you come early we go together.
notes: conditional iḏa; ج→dʒ in jīt; geminate كّ; long vowels; ǧīm→[dʒ] per (watson2002)

gloss_en: I went to the market yesterday and got fish.
notes: past narrative; ق→q (sūq); ج→dʒ in jibt; ta_marbuta; qeltu qāf [q] and ǧīm→[dʒ] per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Tomorrow we travel early in the morning.
notes: pan-Mashriqi bukra; sun assimilation aṣ-ṣ; geminate كّ; emphatic ص; Mashriqi sun-assimilation/emphatic phonology per (watson2002)

gloss_en: Hello, who is with me?
notes: phone frame; pan-Mashriqi mīn 'who' (brustad2000); long vowel

gloss_en: Good morning, how are you?
notes: greeting pair; kīf ḥālak (holes2004); aw retained in al-xayr

gloss_en: Patience is the key to relief.
notes: proverb closing; geminate صّ; emphatic ص; long vowel miftāḥ; Mashriqi emphatic/gemination phonology per (watson2002)
